symptoms: no events returned from Calendar API despite events existing
calendar events not showing up in API response
root_cause: The calendarId parameter is set to 'primary' but the authenticated user/service account does not own the target calendar, or timeMin/timeMax bounds exclude existing events
fix: Use the specific calendar ID (email address of calendar owner or calendar resource ID) instead of 'primary'
Verify timeMin and timeMax are valid RFC3339 timestamps and the range includes the target events
Set singleEvents=true to expand recurring events into individual instances
Check that the service account has been granted access to the calendar via sharing settings

symptoms: cannot create event on shared calendar
insufficient permissions to create calendar event
root_cause: The authenticated identity lacks writer or owner ACL on the target calendar, or the OAuth scope is insufficient (using readonly scope)
fix: Ensure the OAuth token includes the 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar' scope (not the readonly variant)
For shared calendars, verify the user/service account has 'writer' or 'owner' role via calendar ACL
For service accounts, share the calendar with the service account email and grant 'Make changes to events' permission
Check Google Workspace domain policies that may restrict external calendar modifications

symptoms: watch channel created but no notifications arrive
webhooks stopped working after channel expiry
root_cause: The watch channel has expired (max TTL is ~7 days), the receiving endpoint is not HTTPS with a valid certificate, or the domain is not registered in Google Search Console for the project
fix: Register and verify your webhook domain in Google Search Console under the same project
Ensure the receiving URL is publicly reachable over HTTPS with a valid (non-self-signed) certificate
Implement channel renewal by calling events.watch again before the channel's expiration time
Store the channel ID and resource ID to stop expired channels with channels.stop before re-creating

symptoms: event times shifted by several hours
dateTime vs date confusion in event response
root_cause: Timezone not specified in the event start/end objects, or using 'date' (all-day) vs 'dateTime' (timed) incorrectly. All-day events use 'date' field (YYYY-MM-DD) without timezone; timed events require 'dateTime' with an IANA timezone in the 'timeZone' field
fix: For timed events, always set both 'dateTime' (RFC3339) and 'timeZone' (IANA name like 'America/New_York') in start/end objects
For all-day events, use the 'date' field (YYYY-MM-DD format) without 'dateTime' or 'timeZone'
When listing events, use the 'timeZone' query parameter to control how times are returned
Store and compare times in UTC internally; only convert to local timezone for display

root_cause: Exceeding the per-user (per-calendar) rate limit of ~10 QPS or the per-project daily quota. Batch requests count each sub-request individually against quotas
fix: Implement exponential backoff with jitter on 403/429 responses (start at 1s, max 32s)
Use incremental sync (syncToken) instead of polling the full event list repeatedly
Batch related reads together (up to 50 per batch) to reduce round trips but note each sub-request counts toward quota
Cache event data locally and use push notifications to invalidate rather than polling
Request a quota increase in the Google Cloud Console if sustained load requires it

symptoms: modifying single instance of recurring event
root_cause: Recurring events are stored as a single resource with an RRULE. By default, events.list returns the recurring event master, not individual instances. Modifying a single occurrence requires patching the instance, not the master
fix: Set singleEvents=true in events.list to expand recurring events into individual instances sorted by start time
To modify one occurrence, use events.instances to get the specific instance ID, then events.patch on that instance
To create an exception (cancel one occurrence), set the instance's status to 'cancelled'
When creating recurring events, set the 'recurrence' field with RRULE strings (e.g., ['RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO'])

symptoms: how to efficiently sync calendar changes
detecting new or updated events without polling all events
root_cause: Polling the full event list is wasteful and hits rate limits. The Calendar API supports incremental sync via syncToken and pageToken, returning only changes since the last sync
fix: On first sync, call events.list with no syncToken; paginate through all results using nextPageToken
Store the nextSyncToken from the final page of results
On subsequent syncs, pass the stored syncToken to events.list to get only changed/deleted events
If the API returns HTTP 410 (Gone), the syncToken is invalid — perform a full sync again
Combine sync tokens with push notifications: use webhooks to trigger incremental syncs on-demand

root_cause: The If-Match header (or etag in the request body) does not match the current event version. Another client modified the event between your read and write
fix: Always fetch the latest event (events.get) immediately before updating to get the current etag
Use events.patch instead of events.update to send only changed fields, reducing conflict window
Implement retry logic: on 412, re-fetch the event, re-apply your changes, and retry the update
For non-critical updates, omit the If-Match header to force-overwrite (use with caution)

symptoms: event attendees not receiving invitation emails
calendar invitations not sent to guests
root_cause: The sendUpdates parameter defaults to 'none' on events.insert/update/patch, so attendees are not notified unless explicitly requested. Service accounts may also be restricted from sending emails by domain policy
fix: Set sendUpdates='all' to notify all attendees, or sendUpdates='externalOnly' for non-domain guests only
For service accounts using domain-wide delegation, impersonate a real user (set the 'subject' field in JWT) to send invites from their address
Verify the Google Workspace admin has not disabled calendar notifications for the organization
Check that attendees' email addresses are valid and not bouncing

symptoms: create an event from a natural language string
quick add event like 'Lunch tomorrow at noon'
root_cause: events.quickAdd parses a single text string ('Dinner with Sam 7pm Friday') into an event. It is convenient but less precise than events.insert with structured fields.
fix: Call events.quickAdd with calendarId and the text query parameter
Set sendUpdates if you want attendees (parsed from the text) notified
For reliable timezones, titles, and attendees, prefer events.insert with explicit fields
Quick-add interprets the text in the calendar's timezone

symptoms: delete a calendar event via API
remove an event and notify guests
root_cause: events.delete removes an event. Like insert/update, it does not notify attendees unless sendUpdates is set, and deleting an instance of a recurring event differs from deleting the series.
fix: Call events.delete with calendarId and eventId; set sendUpdates='all' to notify guests of the cancellation
Deleting the recurring master removes the whole series
To remove a single occurrence, patch that instance's status to 'cancelled' instead
A 410 Gone means it was already deleted — treat as success

symptoms: move an event to a different calendar
change which calendar an event belongs to
root_cause: events.move transfers an event to another calendar, which also changes the organizer to the destination calendar. You cannot change an event's organizer any other way.
fix: Call events.move with calendarId (source), eventId, and destination (target calendarId)
Both calendars must be accessible to the authenticated identity
Moving is the only supported way to change the organizer of an event
Set sendUpdates to notify attendees of the change

symptoms: the same event appears multiple times after re-import
root_cause: Repeatedly inserting events from an external source duplicates them. events.import upserts by iCalUID, so re-importing the same source event updates rather than duplicates it.
fix: Use events.import (not events.insert) and set a stable iCalUID derived from the source event id
Re-importing with the same iCalUID into the same calendar updates the existing event
events.import is for copying existing events that already have an iCalUID; events.insert mints a new one
Track source-id → eventId mappings as a secondary safeguard

symptoms: patching an event drops the existing attendees
events.patch removed all guests except the one I added
root_cause: events.patch merges top-level fields but replaces array fields (like attendees) entirely. Sending a single attendee in a patch overwrites the whole guest list.
fix: To add one attendee, fetch the current attendees, append, and send the FULL list in the patch
Arrays (attendees, recurrence, attachments) are replaced as a whole, not merged element-wise
Scalar fields (summary, location) can be patched individually
Re-fetch immediately before patching to avoid clobbering concurrent guest changes

symptoms: let a guest accept or decline via API
root_cause: An attendee's RSVP is the responseStatus field (needsAction/accepted/declined/tentative). To change your own RSVP, patch the event with the full attendees array including the updated status.
fix: Patch the event with the complete attendees list, setting the target attendee's responseStatus
The authenticated user can update their own responseStatus on events they're invited to
Changing another guest's status generally requires being the organizer
Send the full attendees array (patch replaces it) to avoid dropping others

symptoms: guests cannot invite others or see the guest list
control what attendees are allowed to do
root_cause: Three event flags govern guest permissions: guestsCanModify, guestsCanInviteOthers (default true), and guestsCanSeeOtherGuests (default true). Set them to lock down an event.
fix: Set guestsCanInviteOthers=false to stop guests adding people
Set guestsCanSeeOtherGuests=false to hide the attendee list from guests
Set guestsCanModify=true to let guests edit the event (overrides the other two)
Apply these on events.insert/patch in the event body

root_cause: An attendee is marked optional by setting optional=true on their entry in the attendees array; otherwise they're treated as required.
fix: Set attendees[].optional=true for non-essential guests
Use attendees[].resource=true for resource (room) entries
Include responseStatus to pre-seed an RSVP if appropriate
Remember patching attendees replaces the whole list — send all of them

symptoms: only some attendees returned for large meetings
root_cause: For events with many guests, the API may limit returned attendees. The maxAttendees parameter caps how many are returned and, when exceeded, sets attendeesOmitted=true.
fix: Pass maxAttendees to control how many attendees are returned per event
Check the attendeesOmitted flag to know the list was truncated
Fetch the single event (events.get) without a tight maxAttendees to get the full list when needed
Very large guest lists are returned in summarized form by design

symptoms: set an event as public or private
root_cause: The visibility field (default/public/private/confidential) controls who can see event details on a shared calendar. It affects detail visibility, not access to the calendar itself.
fix: Set visibility='private' so only attendees/owner see details on an otherwise-shared calendar
visibility='public' exposes details to anyone who can see the calendar
'default' inherits the calendar's default visibility
Visibility is independent of ACL/sharing — it shapes detail exposure, not access

symptoms: show an event as free instead of busy
root_cause: The transparency field controls whether an event consumes the user's availability: 'opaque' (default) marks them busy; 'transparent' marks the time free (e.g., FYI/all-day reminders).
fix: Set transparency='transparent' to keep the time free in free/busy lookups
Leave it 'opaque' (default) for normal busy-blocking meetings
All-day informational events often use transparent so they don't block scheduling
Free/busy queries (freebusy.query) honor this flag

symptoms: store custom app data on an event
attach metadata to events for later lookup
root_cause: extendedProperties let you store custom key/value data on an event: 'private' (visible only to the app on that calendar copy) and 'shared' (visible to all copies/attendees). You can search by privateExtendedProperty.
fix: Set extendedProperties.private or extendedProperties.shared with your key/value pairs on insert/patch
Search later with events.list privateExtendedProperty=key=value (repeatable, AND-ed)
Use 'shared' when attendees' copies must carry the data; 'private' for app-only metadata
Keys/values have size limits; keep them small (store an id, not a blob)

symptoms: set the color of an event
use a specific event color via API
root_cause: Event color is set via colorId, which indexes a fixed palette returned by colors.get (event colors are 1-11). Calendar-level colors are a separate palette.
fix: Fetch the palette once with colors.get; use the event color keys (1-11)
Set event.colorId to the chosen key on insert/patch
Calendar (not event) coloring uses calendarList colorId / backgroundColor instead
Omitting colorId uses the calendar's default color

symptoms: add a popup or email reminder N minutes before
root_cause: Event reminders are set via reminders.overrides (with useDefault=false) where each override has method (popup/email) and minutes; with useDefault=true the calendar's default reminders apply. Max 5 overrides.
fix: Set reminders={useDefault:false, overrides:[{method:'popup',minutes:10}]} on the event
Use method 'email' or 'popup'; at most 5 overrides per event
Set useDefault=true (and omit overrides) to inherit the calendar's defaults
Reminders are per-user; the API sets them on the authenticated user's copy

symptoms: attach a Drive file to an event
add a document attachment to a calendar event
root_cause: Events can carry Drive file attachments via the attachments array, but you must set supportsAttachments=true on the write request or the attachments are ignored.
fix: Set supportsAttachments=true on events.insert/patch and provide attachments[].{fileUrl,title,mimeType}
fileUrl should be the Drive file's alternateLink/URL; the file must be accessible to attendees
Without supportsAttachments=true, the attachments field is silently dropped
There is a limit (currently 25) on attachments per event

symptoms: all-day event ends a day early or spans an extra day
off by one on multi-day all-day events
root_cause: For all-day events the end.date is EXCLUSIVE — a one-day event on the 10th has start.date=2025-01-10 and end.date=2025-01-11. Using the same date for start and end yields a zero-length (invalid) event.
fix: Set end.date to the day AFTER the last day of the event (exclusive bound)
A single all-day event: start.date=D, end.date=D+1
A 3-day event Jan 10-12: start.date=2025-01-10, end.date=2025-01-13
Timed events (dateTime) use an inclusive end instant, unlike all-day date events

symptoms: timeMin/timeMax rejected or ignored in events.list
RFC3339 timestamp must include a timezone offset
root_cause: timeMin and timeMax must be RFC3339 timestamps that include a timezone offset (e.g., 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z or +07:00). Omitting the offset causes errors or unexpected windows.
fix: Always include an explicit offset: use 'Z' for UTC or '+HH:MM' for a zone
Generate timestamps with a library (toISOString() in JS yields a valid 'Z' string)
Do not pass a bare 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS' without offset
timeMin is inclusive and timeMax is exclusive for the query window

symptoms: recurring event creation fails or recurs in the wrong zone
Missing time zone for recurring event
root_cause: A recurring timed event must specify start.timeZone (an IANA name). Without it, the RRULE has no anchor zone and creation fails or recurs incorrectly across DST.
fix: Set start.timeZone and end.timeZone to IANA names (e.g., 'America/New_York') for recurring timed events
Put the RRULE in the recurrence array, e.g. ['RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,WE']
For all-day recurring events use start.date/end.date (no timeZone needed)
DST is handled correctly only when a named timeZone is provided

symptoms: recurring event ends at the wrong time / extra occurrence after DST
root_cause: In an RRULE, the UNTIL value must be a UTC datetime ending in 'Z'. A local-time UNTIL is invalid and can produce an extra or missing final occurrence, especially around DST.
fix: Express UNTIL in UTC: e.g. RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;UNTIL=20250131T235959Z
Convert your intended local end time to UTC before building the UNTIL
Alternatively use COUNT=N instead of UNTIL to bound occurrences
Verify with events.instances that the series ends where expected

symptoms: skip or change specific occurrences of a recurring event
root_cause: Exceptions to a series are handled either by editing a single instance (events.instances + patch), cancelling an instance (status=cancelled), or splitting the series for 'this and following' changes.
fix: To change one occurrence: get it via events.instances, then events.patch that instance id
To skip one occurrence: patch that instance's status to 'cancelled'
For 'this and all following', end the original series (set UNTIL) and create a new recurring event from the change point
The recurrence array can also include EXDATE entries to exclude specific datetimes

symptoms: add a Google Meet link to an event
create a video conference with the event
root_cause: To attach a Google Meet link, send conferenceData.createRequest with a unique requestId AND set the conferenceDataVersion=1 query parameter; without that version param the conference is not created.
fix: Include conferenceDataVersion=1 on the events.insert/patch request (query param)
Set conferenceData.createRequest.requestId to a unique value and conferenceSolutionKey.type='hangoutsMeet'
The Meet link/entryPoints populate asynchronously — read conferenceData.entryPoints after creation
Omitting conferenceDataVersion=1 silently drops the conference request

symptoms: Meet link not present right after creating the event
root_cause: Conference creation is asynchronous: the createRequest returns a status that may be 'pending' before becoming 'success'. Reusing a requestId returns the same conference rather than creating a new one.
fix: After insert, poll events.get (with conferenceDataVersion=1) until conferenceData.createRequest.status.statusCode='success'
Use a fresh, unique requestId for each NEW conference; reusing one is idempotent (returns the same Meet)
Read the join URL from conferenceData.entryPoints (entryPointType='video').uri
Handle 'failure' status by retrying with a new requestId

symptoms: missing events beyond the first page
root_cause: events.list returns at most maxResults events per page (default 250, max 2500). You must follow nextPageToken to retrieve all results.
fix: Loop: keep calling events.list with pageToken=nextPageToken until no nextPageToken is returned
Raise maxResults up to 2500 to reduce round trips
Persist the final nextSyncToken (only present on the last page) for incremental sync
Do not assume a single call returns the full set

symptoms: cannot sort events by start time
root_cause: events.list with orderBy='startTime' requires singleEvents=true, because ordering only makes sense over expanded instances, not recurring masters.
fix: Set singleEvents=true whenever you use orderBy='startTime'
Without singleEvents, only orderBy='updated' is valid
singleEvents=true also expands recurring events into individual instances
Combine with timeMin/timeMax to bound the expansion window

root_cause: The q parameter does a free-text search across event fields (summary, description, location, attendee names/emails, etc.). It is not a structured query language.
fix: Pass q='keyword' to events.list to match across multiple text fields
Combine q with timeMin/timeMax and singleEvents for scoped, ordered results
For exact structured lookups (your app's data), prefer privateExtendedProperty over q
q matching is broad/fuzzy; post-filter results if you need precision

symptoms: deleted events not detected via syncToken
cancelled status and showDeleted in sync
root_cause: In incremental sync, deleted/cancelled events are returned with status='cancelled' rather than being absent. If you filter them out you will miss deletions; you must process cancelled as a removal.
fix: Treat events with status='cancelled' in a sync response as deletions and remove them locally
Do not pass showDeleted=false during incremental sync, or you may miss cancellations
For full (initial) sync, decide whether to include showDeleted based on your needs
Keep applying changes until the page with nextSyncToken is reached

symptoms: how to set up a watch channel for events
subscribe to event changes via webhook
root_cause: events.watch creates a notification channel that POSTs to your HTTPS endpoint on changes. The request body needs id, type='web_hook', address, an optional token, and params.ttl.
fix: Call events.watch with body {id:<uuid>, type:'web_hook', address:'https://...', token:'secret', params:{ttl:'3600'}}
Store the returned resourceId and channel id; you need them to stop the channel
The address must be HTTPS with a valid cert and the domain verified for the project
Channels expire (max ~7 days for events) — renew before expiration

symptoms: how to handle incoming watch notifications
what headers do calendar webhooks send
root_cause: Watch notifications are HTTP POSTs carrying X-Goog headers (Channel-ID, Resource-ID, Resource-State, Message-Number). The body is usually empty — you react by running an incremental sync.
fix: Read X-Goog-Resource-State: 'sync' (initial handshake), 'exists' (a change occurred), or 'not_exists'
Verify X-Goog-Channel-ID and your token match what you stored before acting
On 'exists', trigger an incremental sync (events.list with your stored syncToken) — the POST body has no event data
Respond 200 quickly; do the sync work asynchronously

root_cause: To cancel a notification channel before it expires, call channels.stop with the channel id and resourceId you saved when creating it.
fix: Call channels.stop with body {id:<channelId>, resourceId:<resourceId>}
You must have stored both id and resourceId from the watch response
Stop the old channel before creating a renewed one to avoid duplicate notifications
Channels also auto-expire at their ttl/expiration

symptoms: list all calendars the user can access
root_cause: calendarList.list returns the calendars on the user's list (with per-user settings like color, default reminders, access role). The calendars resource is the underlying calendar metadata, accessed by id.
fix: Call calendarList.list to enumerate calendars the user has added, including accessRole and primary flag
Use the 'primary' entry (or the user's email) for their main calendar
calendars.get(calendarId) returns calendar metadata (timeZone, summary) without user-specific list settings
Page through calendarList with nextPageToken for users with many calendars

symptoms: make an additional calendar via API
root_cause: calendars.insert creates a new secondary calendar owned by the authenticated identity. It returns the new calendarId, which you then use for events.
fix: Call calendars.insert with body {summary:'Team Events', timeZone:'America/New_York'}
Use the returned id as the calendarId for subsequent events/ACL calls
Add it to the user's list and customize per-user settings via calendarList.insert/patch
Share it with others using acl.insert

symptoms: subscribe to an existing or public calendar
add someone else's calendar to my list
root_cause: To follow a calendar you have access to (or a public one), add it to your calendar list with calendarList.insert specifying its id — this is different from creating a new calendar.
fix: Call calendarList.insert with body {id:'<calendarId or public id>'}
You must already have access (it's shared with you or public) for this to succeed
Set per-user options (colorId, defaultReminders, selected) in the same/follow-up call
Use calendarList.delete to unsubscribe (removes from your list, not the calendar itself)

symptoms: share a calendar with a user via API
grant reader/writer access to a calendar
root_cause: Calendar sharing is controlled by the ACL collection. acl.insert grants a scope (user/group/domain) a role: none, freeBusyReader, reader, writer, or owner.
fix: Call acl.insert with {role:'writer', scope:{type:'user', value:'person@example.com'}}
Use 'reader' for view, 'writer' to create/edit events, 'owner' for full control including sharing
Set sendNotifications appropriately when sharing
List/remove access with acl.list / acl.delete using the rule id

symptoms: all-day and recurring events shift after timezone change
root_cause: A calendar has a timeZone that anchors all-day and recurring event interpretation. Changing it (calendars.patch) affects how existing recurring/all-day events are displayed and computed.
fix: Set the timeZone (IANA name) on calendars.insert or update it with calendars.patch
Per-event start.timeZone overrides for timed events; the calendar timeZone is the default anchor
Be cautious changing it on a calendar with recurring events — instances may shift
Read the current value via calendars.get

symptoms: calendars.clear deletes nothing on a secondary calendar
how to remove all events from a calendar
root_cause: calendars.clear wipes all events but only operates on the user's PRIMARY calendar. For a secondary calendar, you must delete events individually or delete and recreate the calendar.
fix: Use calendars.clear only for the primary calendar (calendarId='primary')
For a secondary calendar, page through events.list and call events.delete on each
Or delete the whole secondary calendar with calendars.delete and recreate it
calendars.clear is irreversible — confirm before running

symptoms: 404 Not Found when accessing a calendar by id
root_cause: A 404 on a calendarId usually means the calendar isn't shared with the authenticated identity, the id is wrong, or a special id (with @ or +) wasn't URL-encoded.
fix: Verify the calendar is shared with the user/service account (acl) and use the exact id
URL-encode calendar ids containing special characters (e.g., group ids ending in @group.calendar.google.com)
The primary calendar id is the user's email (or the literal 'primary')
For service accounts, share the calendar or use domain-wide delegation to impersonate an owner

symptoms: check when people are free or busy
root_cause: freebusy.query returns busy time blocks for a set of calendars over a window, without exposing event details — ideal for availability checks and scheduling.
fix: POST to freebusy.query with timeMin, timeMax, and items:[{id:calendarId}, ...]
Read the busy[] intervals per calendar from the response to compute free slots
The caller needs at least freeBusyReader access to each queried calendar
Keep the window reasonable; very large ranges or many calendars increase cost

symptoms: find a meeting time and book a room
schedule across attendees and a resource calendar
root_cause: There is no single 'find meeting time' endpoint in Calendar API v3; you combine freebusy.query across attendees and room (resource) calendars, pick a slot, then create the event adding the room as a resource attendee.
fix: Run freebusy.query including attendee calendars and the room's resource calendar id
Compute a common free slot from the returned busy intervals
Create the event with the room added as an attendee (resource=true) so it gets booked
Resource (room) calendars come from the Workspace directory; the room auto-accepts if free

symptoms: service account cannot create events on a user's calendar
service account event has wrong organizer
Calendar requires domain-wide delegation to act as a user
root_cause: A service account only has its own calendar by default. To create events on a user's calendar or send invites from their address, it needs domain-wide delegation and must impersonate that user.
fix: Enable domain-wide delegation and authorize the SA client id with the calendar scope in the Admin Console
Impersonate the user by setting the JWT 'subject' (sub) to their email when minting credentials
Alternatively, have the user share their calendar with the SA email and grant 'Make changes to events'
To send invitations from the user's address, impersonation (DWD) is required, not just sharing

symptoms: add a Workspace resource/room to an event
root_cause: Rooms are Workspace resource calendars. You book one by adding its resource email as an attendee (resource=true); if the room is free it auto-accepts.
fix: Find the room's resource email via the Admin SDK Directory or the resource calendar id
Add it to attendees with {email:'<room>@resource.calendar.google.com', resource:true}
Check the room's free/busy first (freebusy.query) to avoid conflicts
The room's responseStatus reflects whether the booking was accepted

symptoms: create out-of-office, focus time, or working location events
root_cause: Beyond default events, Calendar supports special eventTypes: outOfOffice, focusTime, and workingLocation. Set eventType on insert and provide the matching properties object.
fix: Set eventType='outOfOffice' (with outOfOfficeProperties) to auto-decline meetings during that time
Use eventType='focusTime' (focusTimeProperties) to mark concentration blocks
Use eventType='workingLocation' (workingLocationProperties) to set home/office/custom location
These types have constraints (e.g., often single-attendee/own-calendar) — see the docs per type

symptoms: floating times shift for attendees in other timezones
meeting appears at different local times correctly but data looks off
store events in UTC with timeZone
root_cause: Timed events store an instant (dateTime + timeZone). Each attendee sees it in their own zone. Problems arise when clients strip the timeZone or assume a single local zone.
fix: Always send dateTime WITH a timeZone (or a UTC 'Z' offset) so the instant is unambiguous
Store/compare events as UTC internally; convert to local only for display
Use the events.list 'timeZone' parameter to control the zone times are returned in
Never drop the offset/timeZone — that creates floating-time ambiguity

symptoms: get the individual occurrences of one recurring event
list instances of a specific series
root_cause: events.instances returns the expanded occurrences of a single recurring event (by its master eventId), including any modified or cancelled instances — distinct from events.list with singleEvents.
fix: Call events.instances with calendarId and the recurring event's eventId
Use timeMin/timeMax to bound which occurrences are returned
Modified occurrences appear with their own eventId and originalStartTime
Cancelled occurrences appear with status='cancelled'

symptoms: identifying which series a modified instance belongs to
linking instances back to the master
root_cause: A modified occurrence carries recurringEventId (the master's id) and originalStartTime (its slot in the series). These let you relate instances to their series and detect moved occurrences.
fix: Read recurringEventId on an instance to find its master event
Use originalStartTime to know which occurrence it represents, even if its start was moved
When syncing, key instances by recurringEventId + originalStartTime for stable identity
A null recurringEventId means it's a standalone (non-recurring) event

symptoms: attendees added via patch don't get the right notifications
root_cause: sendUpdates controls who is emailed on create/update/delete/move: 'all' notifies everyone, 'externalOnly' only non-Workspace-domain guests, and 'none' (default) notifies nobody.
fix: Pass sendUpdates='all' when you want every attendee notified of changes
Use 'externalOnly' to email only guests outside your Workspace domain
Default 'none' means silent changes — set it explicitly when invites are expected
Service accounts may need DWD impersonation of a real user to actually send mail

symptoms: set default reminders for an entire calendar
change reminders applied to all events by default
root_cause: Per-user default reminders live on the calendarList entry (defaultReminders), not on the calendar resource. Events with reminders.useDefault=true inherit these.
fix: Patch the calendarList entry with defaultReminders:[{method:'popup',minutes:30}]
Events with reminders.useDefault=true then inherit these defaults for that user
Per-event overrides (useDefault=false) take precedence over calendar defaults
defaultReminders are per-user and per-calendar (on the list entry)

symptoms: updating an event accidentally clears fields I didn't send
root_cause: events.update performs a full replace: any writable field you omit is cleared to its default. Use events.patch to change only specific fields and preserve the rest.
fix: Use events.patch to modify a subset of fields without clearing others
If you must use events.update, fetch the event first and send back the complete resource with your changes
Remember even with patch, array fields (attendees) are replaced wholesale
Prefer patch + conditional retry for safe partial edits

symptoms: read created/updated timestamps and sequence of an event
detect when an event was last modified
root_cause: Each event exposes created and updated timestamps and an iCalendar 'sequence' number that increments on significant changes — useful for change detection and conflict reasoning.
fix: Read event.updated (RFC3339) to know the last modification time
Use event.sequence to track revisions; it must be preserved/incremented on update
Combine updated timestamps with syncToken-based sync rather than polling all events
created is set once at insert and does not change

symptoms: birthdays or holidays events are read-only
cannot edit events on holiday/birthday calendars
root_cause: Google-generated calendars (Holidays, Birthdays/Contacts, Week numbers) are read-only. Attempts to insert/update/delete their events fail; you can only read them.
fix: Treat holiday/birthday calendars as read-only sources; do not attempt writes
To add custom events, create them on the user's primary or a secondary calendar instead
You can still read these calendars (events.list) for display/aggregation
Subscribe/unsubscribe via calendarList rather than editing their contents

symptoms: add a structured location or address to an event
root_cause: The event location is a single free-text string. Calendar may render a map if it recognizes the address, but there is no separate structured-address object for standard events.
fix: Set event.location to a human-readable address string on insert/patch
Provide a complete, geocodable address if you want Calendar/Maps to link it
For virtual meetings, prefer conferenceData over stuffing a URL into location
Room bookings use resource attendees, not the location string
